adr_findings.evidence: normalizeImpactedDomains (internal/validate/ownership_resolve.go) resolves a path-like Impacted-Domains entry ONLY by glob-matching it against every domain's EXPLICIT per-domain OWNERSHIP.yaml paths: (loadOwnershipForRef + matchesAny minus Exclude). It consumes declared data + explicit manifests and performs NO framework inference: it never re-synthesizes the removed `internal/<domain>/**` fallback, never does path-PREFIX guessing. Zero-owner and >1-owner are HARD errors (impacted-domains-resolve), not a synthesized default. Rule 2 keeps a bare-name token verbatim (legacy named-domain) and explicitly does NOT invent an error/owner for it. Domain enumeration reuses resolveDomains/listDomainDirs and is ref-anchored when ownerRef is set (consistent with spec-095 ADR-0036 amendment). ZFC-clean reading is exactly as the ADR-0032 amendment narrates (lines 156-159).

Amendment 'Impacted-Domains normalization (2026-06-16, spec 100)' present (lines 131-164). (a) `workflow` added to the Domain(s) line (line 5: validation, adr, lifecycle, workflow). (b) Dated note amends sub-decision 1: path-like entries are NORMALIZED to owning-domain dir-name when exactly one domain's paths: glob claims them, ERROR on zero/>1 — softening the original outright rejection of path-like identifiers while keeping the canonical dir-name as the compared identifier. Accurate vs code: helper signature, the keep-verbatim/glob-resolve/zero-or-multi-error rule, and the preserved per-file attribution + blast-radius guard all match. Status still Accepted (line 4). Mirrors the 099->ADR-0037 amendment pattern (spec.md L122 states intent explicitly).

R3's adrReadStore (cmd/mindspec/adr.go) reads ADRs only through the adr.Store interface — adr.NewOverlayStore(adr.NewFileStore(localRoot), adr.NewFileStore(mainRoot)) — the file-based default backend, branch-over-main, identical to the validator's adrStoreForSpec (internal/validate/plan.go:240). NewOverlayStore(branch, primary) arg order is correct (overlaystore.go:24 'branch wins on ID conflicts'): worktree-local ADRs win, main-only ADRs stay visible. In a plain checkout FindLocalRoot==FindRoot so the overlay collapses to a single FileStore. parse.go unchanged (test-only lock). No direct os.ReadFile of ADR files; consumers stay backend-agnostic.

summary: All four lenses pass. (1) ADR-0036: normalize-at-source is genuinely ZFC-clean — it resolves declared Impacted-Domains entries against EXPLICIT OWNERSHIP.yaml paths: globs only, with hard errors on zero/ambiguous owners and NO synthesized internal/<domain>/** fallback, so it does not reintroduce the violation 0036 removed. (2) ADR-0032 is properly amended: workflow added to Domain(s), a dated note softens sub-decision 1's path-rejection to normalize-or-error, accurate vs code, Status still Accepted, mirroring the 099->0037 pattern. (3) ADR-0024: R3's overlay show/list reads through the Store interface (NewOverlayStore/NewFileStore) exactly as the validator does. (4) No code change contradicts a cited ADR without amending it — the only contradiction (sub-decision 1) is the one this spec amends. The plan-path nil-executor call to normalizeImpactedDomains is safe: ownerRef=='' routes resolveDomains/loadOwnershipForRef to the on-disk LoadOwnership/listDomainDirs branch that never touches exec, mirroring attributeDomain's documented plumbing.
